trade.summary: Substantial bilateral trade. Turchia exports vehicles, machinery, textiles, and food products to the Stati Uniti; the Stati Uniti exports aircraft, defence equipment, chemicals, and machinery to Turchia. Trade has been affected by tariff disputes (2018 steel/aluminium tariffs) but has largely recovered.
disputes: 2018 Stati Uniti steel and aluminium tariffs on Turchia (partially resolved)
CAATSA sanctions threat over S-400 purchase

diplomatic.summary: Turchia and the Stati Uniti are formal NATO allies but have experienced significant strains over multiple issues: Turchia's purchase of the Russian S-400 air defence system, which led to Turchia's removal from the F-35 programme; diverging positions on Kurdish forces in Syria; Turchia's partial accommodation of Russia despite NATO membership; and President Erdogan's adversarial relationship with successive Stati Uniti administrations. The alliance persists but trust has significantly eroded.
recent_incidents: Turchia removed from F-35 programme after S-400 purchase (2019)
Stati Uniti imposed CAATSA sanctions on Turchia's defence procurement agency (2020)
Turchia's blocking of Svezia and Finlandia's initial NATO applications (2022-2024)
Divergence on Syria policy regarding Kurdish YPG/SDF forces

military.summary: Formal NATO allies with Turchia hosting the Incirlik Air Base — a critical NATO asset — and serving as guardian of the Turkish Straits. However, Turchia's S-400 purchase led to its expulsion from the F-35 programme and CAATSA sanctions. Military cooperation continues through NATO frameworks but bilateral trust is damaged. Turchia is the only NATO member that refused to apply Russia sanctions unanimously. Joint exercises continue under NATO auspices.

financial.summary: Moderate financial ties. Stati Uniti firms have significant Turchia investments in manufacturing, finance, and energy. Turchia holds Stati Uniti Treasury bonds as part of FX reserves. The Turkish lira crisis of 2018 was partly triggered by Stati Uniti-Turchia political tensions. Stati Uniti investors maintain exposure through emerging market funds.

energy.summary: Growing Stati Uniti LNG exports to Turchia as Turchia diversifies away from Russian gas post-2022. Stati Uniti energy firms are active in Turchia's upstream sector. Turchia's role as an energy transit hub is strategically important to the Stati Uniti. Turchia participates in the Southern Gas Corridor favoured by the Stati Uniti.

scientific.summary: Moderate scientific collaboration through academic exchanges, Fulbright programmes, and joint research. Turkish universities have strong Stati Uniti partnerships. Bilateral STEM collaboration has continued despite political tensions. Brain drain of Turkish scientists to the Stati Uniti has increased.
